---
title: "Week 7 — Simple regression"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

How do we model a numerical outcome?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Simple linear regression as both prediction and association; slope,
intercept, residuals, and prediction limits.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Interpret a regression output table (no R required) and write a slope
sentence in context.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab; short prediction-and-limitations
scenario.

## Primary source basis

OpenIntro IMS Ch 7 (model-slr) and Ch 24 (inf-model-slr).

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Health/life-science prediction vignettes; instructor-provided
regression output.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Interpret slope and intercept in context.
- [ ] Use a regression line for basic prediction.
- [ ] Describe residuals conceptually.
- [ ] Avoid causal overclaims.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
